report_name: CISA Adds Two Actively Exploited Joomla Plugin Flaws to KEV Catalog

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting unrestricted file upload vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-48939 and CVE-2026-56291) in public-facing Joomla components to achieve arbitrary code execution.

attack_patterns.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: Exploitation of the Joomla plugin vulnerabilities typically results in the installation of a webshell to maintain persistence and execute arbitrary code on the compromised server.

malware.name: Webshell
malware.description: A webshell is deployed following the successful exploitation of the unrestricted file upload vulnerabilities in Joomla plugins iCagenda and Balbooa Forms, allowing for arbitrary code execution.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-48939
vulnerabilities.description: An unrestricted file upload vulnerability in the iCagenda extension for Joomla allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code by uploading a file with a malicious extension, such as .php.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-56291
vulnerabilities.description: An unrestricted file upload vulnerability in the Balbooa Forms component for Joomla allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code by uploading a file with a malicious extension, such as .php.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
identities.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added two Joomla vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog due to active exploitation.

identities.name: Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies
identities.description: A class of U.S. government organizations mandated by CISA to remediate the exploited Joomla vulnerabilities by a specified deadline.

indicators.name: Suspicious PHP File Creation in Joomla Directories
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious PHP File Creation in Joomla Directories
id: 2b1a9c8d-3e4f-4a1b-9c8d-2b1a9c8d3e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of PHP files in common Joomla web-accessible directories, which could indicate exploitation of an unrestricted file upload vulnerability like CVE-2026-48939 or CVE-2026-56291.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/07/10/cisa-adds-two-known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.php'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '/components/com_icagenda/'
      - '/components/com_balbooaforms/'
      - '/images/'
      - '/media/'
  condition: selection_ext and 1 of selection_path
indicators.description: This rule detects the creation of new PHP files within Joomla component or media directories. This behavior is a strong indicator of a webshell being uploaded after exploiting a file upload vulnerability, such as those found in iCagenda (CVE-2026-48939) and Balbooa Forms (CVE-2026-56291).

relationships.source_name: Suspicious PHP File Creation in Joomla Directories
